title: Normalized Feed Domain, Content Variants, and Provenance · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Content Kind
summary: RSS and Atom transport structs leak into storage, missing data is invented, HTML and text are conflated, source identifiers are discarded, and updates cannot be explained. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go content kind increment in loss-aware normalized feed and entry model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Preserve plain text, escaped HTML, XHTML, external content, summary, and missing content as distinct bounded states.
Choose entry identity from stable Atom ID, RSS GUID semantics, canonical link, or scoped content fingerprint with explicit precedence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
